content: Date 2026-07-01. I'm Sam, 31, backend/ML engineer at Cairn (Paris logistics startup). Quick half-year review out loud so I can decide next steps. Tech: Cairn shipped the beta on 2026-06-15 to 40 pilot customers — I pushed the new ranking model to prod 2026-05-20 and we saw ETA variance drop ~12%. That felt good; 3 months of work from March–May. Health: knee setback — twisted it April 28, MRI done 2026-05-05 (€300), diagnosis partial meniscus tear. Physio 2x/week (Tue/Thu 18:30), €45/session -> €90/week, ortho follow-up planned 2026-07-15 (€70). I'm avoiding surgery for now but pain flared last week doing a squat. I want to avoid surgery if possible; deadline for deciding is 2026-09-01. Housing: apartment hunt got serious since 2026-06-01 — I've seen 12 flats, two favourites in the 10th and 11th arrondissements. Budget €1,600 net/month, deposit €4,800 saved. Target move-in 2026-09-01. Money: salary €72,000 gross/yr, savings €24,000, monthly burn €2,200; 6-month emergency = €13,200. Rehab + moving costs estimated additional €1,200 by Sep 1. Decisions I need to make now: 1) Prioritise knee recovery vs pushing hard on Cairn GA (goal end Q3 2026 ≈ 2026-09-30). 2) How much to commit to the apartment (offer deadline maybe by 2026-07-10). 3) Work accommodations — limit hours, standing desk, defer travel. I want to think through trade-offs and lock a plan.
Which single item is top priority right now: knee recovery to avoid surgery, locking the apartment by 2026-09-01, or supporting Cairn through GA on 2026-09-30?

Top priority is knee recovery (avoid surgery). Second is locking the apartment for 2026-09-01. Third is supporting Cairn GA 2026-09-30 but at reduced capacity. Concretely: I want to limit to 30 hours/week until 2026-09-30, keep core deliverables, and avoid travel/overnights. Doable with manager support? I also want to keep the 6-month emergency buffer €13,200 from the €24k savings and use a relocation pot €5k for deposit + move.
Can you accept reduced hours without prorated pay (i.e., same salary/vesting) or do you need a salary adjustment?

I want salary and vesting unchanged for now. I'll use savings to keep the €13,200 buffer and €5,000 as the relocation + deposit source (deposit €4,800 + ~€1,000 moving buffer). Rehab costs: €90/week physio, ortho follow-ups ~€70/month, so estimate ~€1,200 extra to 2026-09-01. I met parents for dinner 2026-06-20 to update them — they're supportive. Next concrete steps I want: manager meeting 2026-07-02 to request accommodations, standing desk delivered by 2026-07-10, defer on-call/travel until 2026-10-01, and a surgery decision cutoff 2026-09-01. I need a checklist to present and an email draft for the manager.
Confirm checklist sections: work (email + calendar + accommodations), health (appointments + rehab milestones), housing (applications + budget + offer timeline) — is that right?

Yes, exactly. Add dates: manager meeting 2026-07-02, standing desk 2026-07-10, on-call/travel paused until 2026-10-01, physio Tue/Thu 18:30, ortho follow-up 2026-07-15, apartment offer target by 2026-07-10 if landlord responds. Tone of the manager message should be collaborative. Keep it concise and bullet-style, with the exact items: 30 hours/week until 2026-09-30, core hours 10:00–16:00 Mon–Fri, no travel/overnights until 2026-10-01, standing desk by 2026-07-10, salary and vesting unchanged, surgery decision by 2026-09-01 if no improvement. Also cc HR. Can you draft that email and make a 10-item checklist with due dates between 2026-07-01 and 2026-09-01?
Do you want the email sent immediately after I draft it or reviewed first by you before sending (I can add inline dates/numbers)?
